While speaking of tank construction our author states3 that
tanks should have long sides on the east and west. He gives a recipe4
for purifying well-water. For rain-water Vardha's authority is Baladeva^
but Utpala does not quote him. On the other hand, he quotes from
the works offlsiputm, Garga, Devala and K&iyapa. So we are in the
dark about the work of Baladeva, which was available to the
author.
It is felt that Chapters LVI to LX should have been arranged
after LIII, but the author gives the reason for the change in LVI. 1,
According to Maya and ViSvakarman the height of a temple-storey
should he 108 and 84 digits respectively. Maya is the authority
mentioned by our author for his treatment of adamantine glue.5 In
LVIII we are given units of lineal measurements: Paramd^u (Atom),
Rajas (Dust-particle), Vdldgra (Tip of hair), a Nit, Louse, Barley, and
digit, each following measure being eight times the previous one,
